perturbation.original_text: Either party may terminate this Agreement at any time by written notice to the other party. Upon such termination or other expiration of this Agreement, the Receiving Party will return all of the Confidential Information in written or other tangible form, including any copies made, to the Disclosing Party together with certification that any other copies or notes or summaries (to the extent i ncluding the Confidential Information) have been destroyed.
perturbation.changed_text: Either party may terminate this Agreement at any time by written notice to the other party. Upon such termination or other expiration of this Agreement, the Receiving Party will retain all of the Confidential Information in written or other tangible form, including any copies made, unless otherwise instructed in writing by the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: The original section requires the Receiving Party to return all Confidential Information upon termination. The modified version allows the Receiving Party to retain the information unless specifically instructed otherwise, contradicting the initial obligation to return everything. This in-text contradiction creates ambiguity over what happens to the Confidential Information post-termination, completely undermining the purpose of section 4.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Either party may terminate this Agreement at any time by written notice to the other party. Upon such termination or other expiration of this Agreement, the Receiving Party will retain all of the Confidential Information in written or other tangible form, including any copies made, unless otherwise instructed in writing by the Disclosing Party. The provisions of Sections 1 t hrough 5 of this Agreement shall survive any termination or expiration.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires the Receiving Party to return all Confidential Information upon termination, while the changed text allows them to retain it unless instructed otherwise. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, further highlighting the contradiction created by the modification. This creates a direct and irreconcilable conflict within the contract regarding the disposition of confidential information upon termination, constituting a strong in-text contradiction as defined by the Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The parties acknowledges that a Disclosing Party will be irreparably harmed if the Receiving Party's obligat ions under this Agreement are not specifically enforced and that the Disclosing Party would not have an adequate remedy at law in the event of an actual or threatened violati on by the Receiving Party of the Receiving Party's obligations. Therefore, in addition to all other remedies it may have, the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to an injunction or any app ropriate decree of specific performance for any actual or threatened violations or breaches by the Receiving Party or the Receiving Party's employees or agents without the necessity o f the Disclosing Party showing actual damages or that monetary damages would not afford a n adequate remedy, and without posting a bond.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties acknowledges that a Disclosing Party may not be irreparably harmed if the Receiving Party's obligations under this Agreement are not specifically enforced and that the Disclosing Party may have an adequate remedy at law in the event of an actual or threatened violation by the Receiving Party of the Receiving Party's obligations. Therefore, the Disclosing Party may not be entitled to an injunction or any appropriate decree of specific performance for any actual or threatened violations or breaches by the Receiving Party or the Receiving Party's employees or agents, and may be required to show actual damages or that monetary damages would not afford an adequate remedy, and may be required to post a bond.
perturbation.explanation: This modification directly contradicts the original clause by stating that the Disclosing Party 'may not' be irreparably harmed and 'may not' be entitled to injunctive relief. This contradicts the original assertion that the Disclosing Party 'will be irreparably harmed' and 'shall be entitled to an injunction'. The explicit contradiction creates uncertainty about the availability of injunctive relief, potentially undermining the Disclosing Party's ability to protect its Confidential Information effectively. The in-text contradiction about the entitlement to injunction is clearly placed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: I NJUNCTIVE RELIEF . The parties acknowledges that a Disclosing Party may not be irreparably harmed if the Receiving Party's obligations under this Agreement are not specifically enforced and that the Disclosing Party may have an adequate remedy at law in the event of an actual or threatened violation by the Receiving Party of the Receiving Party's obligations. Therefore, the Disclosing Party may not be entitled to an injunction or any appropriate decree of specific performance for any actual or threatened violations or breaches by the Receiving Party or the Receiving Party's employees or agents, and may be required to show actual damages or that monetary damages would not afford an adequate remedy, and may be required to post a bond.
perturbation.justification: The Changed Text directly contradicts the Contradicted Text. The Original Text states the Disclosing Party 'will be irreparably harmed' and 'shall be entitled to an injunction'. The Changed Text then modifies this to state the Disclosing Party 'may not be irreparably harmed' and 'may not be entitled to injunctive relief'. The Contradicted Text is a direct copy of this changed text, therefore confirming that a structural flaw in-text contradiction has been introduced as defined in the perturbation definition.

perturbation.original_text: Neither party shall assign or otherwise transfer any of its rights or obligations under this Agreement to any third pa rty without the prior written consent of the other party
perturbation.changed_text: Either party may assign or otherwise transfer any of its rights or obligations under this Agreement to any third party, irrespective of the written consent of the other party.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the requirement for prior written consent for assignment of rights or obligations. The new wording explicitly permits either party to assign their rights without the other party's consent, undermining the original restriction. This in-text contradiction creates uncertainty about whether consent is required for assignment, making the clause unenforceable and creating ambiguity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Neither party may assign or otherwise transfer any of its rights or obligations under this Agreement to any third party, irrespective of the written consent of the other party; failure by a party to enforce any provisions of this Agreement at any time 3 shall in no manner affect the right of that party a t a later time to enforce any provision of this Agreement; this Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of England without reference to the principles of conflict of laws; in the event that any word, phrase, clause, sentence or other provision h erein shall violate any applicable statute, ordinance or rule of law in any jurisdiction which governs this Agreement, such provisions shall be effective to the extent of such violation without invalidating any other provision herein; this Agreement supersedes all previous unde rstandings or agreements between the parties and incorporates the entire agreement of th e parties with respect to the receipt and use of the Confidential Information; this Agreement may only be amended by a writing of subsequent date that is signed by both parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text prohibits assignment without prior written consent. The changed text explicitly allows assignment irrespective of consent. The contradicted text mirrors the original text's prohibition. Therefore, the changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text by removing the requirement for consent, creating a strong in-text contradiction as the contradicted text still enforces a clause that the changed text invalidates.

perturbation.original_text: this Agreement may only be amended by a writing of subsequent date that is signed by both parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be amended orally, and such oral amendments shall supersede any prior written agreements.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the clause requiring written amendments signed by both parties. By allowing oral amendments to supersede prior written agreements, the written amendment requirement is nullified. This in-text contradiction creates significant uncertainty about the validity of any amendments, as it is now unclear whether they must be written or can be oral.
perturbation.contradicted_text: this Agreement supersedes all previous unde rstandings or agreements between the parties and incorporates the entire agreement of th e parties with respect to the receipt and use of the Confidential Information; This Agreement may be amended orally, and such oral amendments shall supersede any prior written agreements. IN WITNESS WHEREOF , the parties have executed this Agreement as of th e date set forth above.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly allows oral amendments to supersede prior written agreements, which directly contradicts the original requirement for written amendments signed by both parties. The contradicted text contains the changed text as well, which means there is an in-text contradiction. This aligns with the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' definition, as the clause is placed in the same location with conflicting implications. A reasonable reader would immediately recognize these sections as incompatible.
